phase_name: Connes proof — cross-term cancellation mechanism identified
task: Formalize cross-term cancellation proof: D null vectors => D^2 cross terms => eps_0 ~ 1/D

completed_tasks.name: eps_0 < min(M eig) via W02 cross-term cancellation

remaining_tasks.name: Formalize cross-term cancellation: D null vectors => eps_0 ~ 1/D^alpha
Compute the cross-term sum explicitly and verify 1/D scaling
Write the complete proof argument combining all pieces

decisions.decision: Near-null M eigenvalues ~10^{-8} constant, don't decrease with N
decisions.rationale: Measured at N=15..50 for lam^2=200, and N=31..61 for lam^2=50..2000

decisions.decision: eps_0 < min(M eig) via W02 rank-2 cross-term cancellation
decisions.rationale: eps_0~10^{-10} while min(M)~10^{-8}: cross terms provide extra 10^2 factor

decisions.decision: More null-space vectors = more cross terms = better cancellation
decisions.rationale: Growing D = dim-26 provides D^2/2 cross terms for cancellation

context_notes: The proof mechanism is fully identified: (1) M has rank~BW~26 (sampling theorem), (2) null space grows as 2N-25, (3) near-null eigenvalues ~10^{-8} constant, (4) eps_0 beaten below 10^{-8} by W02 cross-term cancellation using D null-space vectors, (5) more vectors = better cancellation. Next: formalize steps 4-5 as rigorous bounds.
next_action: Compute the cross-term sum explicitly: sum_{k in null, l=top} c_k c_l W02_{kl}. Verify it scales as ~0.03 with residual ~1/D. This would complete the proof chain.
